mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near historic lows (sub-1%) signals persistently accommodative monetary policy that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing a structural positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the low-rate regime persists.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during periods when FEDFUNDS < 1%, or mean 20-day forward return under this condition is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate relative to its recent trailing low (Sahm-rule style trigger) signals an emerging labor market downturn that precedes broad economic contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by >= 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 3-month minimum
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical trigger instances, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows conditioned on the trigger being active.

mechanism_narrative: When the nominal federal funds rate rises faster than trailing CPI inflation, the implied real policy rate turns restrictive, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns; the effect requires the joint behavior of both rate and inflation series, not either alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL percent change exceeds +2 percentage points (real rate positive and rising)
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return conditioned on positive/rising real rate is not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are entered as separate univariate regressors (no incremental interaction signal).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp three-month decline in housing starts signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand that is most predictive of equity weakness specifically when monetary policy is in a tightening posture, since housing is the primary transmission channel of rate hikes into real activity.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% over the trailing 3 months
failure_condition: The negative return relationship fails to hold (t-stat insignificant at 5% level) when the trigger is active during tightening regimes, or the effect is equally strong during easing regimes, invalidating regime-conditionality.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in industrial production reflects expanding real output capacity and corporate earnings power, generating a persistent short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns as production trends confirm economic strength.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips across more than one full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of negative month-over-month retail sales growth signal a deceleration in consumer spending, the dominant driver of GDP, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth is negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following the trigger are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth below a 1% annualized 3-month pace and a concurrent month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate jointly indicate a genuine labor market inflection rather than noise, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; neither series alone reliably signals the turn.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 1% AND UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint-trigger negative return effect is no stronger than either single-series trigger tested independently, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) when both conditions hold.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration of year-over-year CPI inflation above 3% raises the probability of near-term restrictive monetary policy surprises, producing a short-horizon negative reaction in equity prices as markets reprice rate-hike expectations.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return following the trigger is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or is indistinguishable from the unconditional baseline return distribution.
